description: Deadline and negotiability configuration. Controls how the skill infers deadlines, computes negotiability from weekday gaps, and escalates overdue tasks. Load on-demand during planning (Step 3.5) and rebalancing (Mode 2).

metadata_format.location: Last line of TickTick task content (description) field
rules: Append after existing description content, preceded by a blank line. Never overwrite existing text.
If description is empty, the deadline line is the only content.
The reason after | is human-readable — Carlos should understand why this date matters at a glance.
If a task has no inferable deadline, leave the description clean. No deadline line = fully movable.
NEVER add deadline metadata to recurring tasks (they affect all future occurrences).
Time component (HH:MM) is optional. Use it only for meeting-anchored tasks where the specific time matters.
Date and time are always in Europe/Berlin local time, NOT UTC.

gap_computation.rule: gap = count_weekdays(today, deadline). Weekdays = Mon–Fri only. Weekends and the current day are excluded from the count. Example: Friday → Monday = 1 weekday. Friday → Friday next week = 5 weekdays. Thursday → Monday = 1 weekday.

negotiability_thresholds.description: Negotiability is computed dynamically from the weekday gap. Never stored as a static label.

levels.label: freely_movable
levels.rebalance_behavior: Defer without asking Carlos. First candidates for displacement when something urgent lands.

levels.label: movable_flagged
levels.rebalance_behavior: Can move, but flag in the rebalance plan: '⚠️ getting close to deadline'. Carlos sees it but doesn't need to approve.

levels.label: resist_moving
levels.rebalance_behavior: Ask Carlos before moving. Show the deadline and reason: 'This is due [date] for [reason]. Still okay to push?' Never auto-defer.

levels.label: immovable
levels.rebalance_behavior: Never defer. If it can't fit today, escalate: 'This is due today — what should give way?'

no_deadline.label: unanchored
no_deadline.rebalance_behavior: Most movable pool. Deferred first during rebalancing. TickTick priority determines order within this pool (HIGH deferred last, NONE deferred first).

overdue_escalation.trigger: Task has a deadline line and gap < 0 (deadline date has passed)
overdue_escalation.behavior: Surface once per planning cycle with a quick-resolve prompt via AskUserQuestion
overdue_escalation.prompt_template: ⏰ OVERDUE: '{task_title}' was due {deadline_date} ({abs_gap} weekdays ago) for: {reason}
overdue_escalation.after_resolution: Update the deadline line in the task description with the new date, or remove it if dropped. Do not re-flag until the new deadline is also overdue.
